_comment: Polynuclear phosphorus oxoacid table - Stage 6 R2-F.
Maps RDKit-canonical SMILES of standalone polynuclear phosphorus
oxoacids (P-O-P chains, direct-P-P bonds) to the OPSIN-accepted
preferred IUPAC name.  Keys are RDKit canonical SMILES obtained
via Chem.MolToSmiles after parsing the OPSIN reference SMILES
from nonCarboxylicAcids.xml.  Values are the single preferred
IUPAC name for that standalone molecule; the engine looks up via
whole-molecule canonical SMILES (same dispatch pattern as R1-B's
heteroelement_oxoacids.json).
Source: OPSIN 2.8.0 nonCarboxylicAcids.xml.  Every entry has been
probed with py2opsin for round-trip (name -> SMILES -> same
RDKit canonical) before inclusion.  See tmp_probes/s6_r2_f.
Not in scope (and intentionally absent):
- mononuclear phosphoric / phosphonic acid: covered by existing
_INORGANIC_CURATED_SMILES and R1-B heteroelement_oxoacids
tables respectively.
- phosphoronitridic acid (N#P(O)O): covered by R1-F
acid_infix_composition.
- fully deprotonated diphosphate/triphosphate/hypodiphosphate
anions: already round-trip via the ordinary descriptive
phosphanediylbis(oxidanide) assembly.
- cyclotriphosphoric / metaphosphoric acid: OPSIN 2.8.0 cannot
parse either name, so no round-trip verification is possible.
_subsumed_note: acid) were REMOVED: the generative maingroup_oxoacids.compute_name
reproduces them exactly from structural features (P-O-P anhydride
chain -> di/tri prefix; direct P-P -> hypodi).  This lookup table and
its engine dispatch hook are now redundant; the module is retained
only as an empty placeholder so existing imports do not break.
